prerequisites: An AWS account with an Amazon SQS queue already created (the queue's full endpoint URL is required).
An AWS IAM user with an access key ID and secret access key, and permissions to read from the target SQS queue.
The AWS region code where the SQS queue lives (for example eu-west-1).
The full SQS queue URL, for example https://sqs.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/1234567890/example-queue-url.

authMethods.name: AWS IAM access key (access_key + secret_key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an AWS IAM access key ID and secret access key belonging to a user that has permission to read the target SQS queue. Access keys consist of an access key ID (e.g. AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE) and a secret access key; both are used together to sign API requests. The secret access key is shown only once at creation, so save it securely. The connector maps these to the access_key and secret_key fields.

setupSteps.title: Create an SQS queue and copy its URL
setupSteps.body: In the AWS console create the Amazon SQS queue you want to read from, then copy its full endpoint URL (e.g. https://sqs.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/1234567890/example-queue-url). Use this for the queue_url field and note the queue's region for the region field (e.g. eu-west-1).

setupSteps.title: Create AWS IAM access keys
setupSteps.body: Create an IAM user (do not use root credentials) with permission to receive messages from the queue, then generate an access key pair under the user's security credentials. Save the access key ID and secret access key, since the secret is only retrievable at creation time. Enter them into the access_key and secret_key fields.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector fields
setupSteps.body: Provide the required queue_url, region, access_key, and secret_key. Optionally set target, and tune the polling and message behavior: max_batch_size (max messages per poll, 1-10, default 10), max_wait_time (max poll time in seconds, 1-20, default 20), visibility_timeout (seconds a message stays hidden after being read, default 20), and attributes_to_return (comma separated list of message attributes to return, default All).
